builds.name: The Long Burn
builds.core: Firewall (Ring of Fire), Pyromancy skills to keep Heat maxed. Sustained DoT pressure, positioning over reflexes.
builds.narrator: Some players don't want to swing for the fences. They'd rather build something that just doesn't stop. Meet **The Long Burn**.

builds.name: The Comet
builds.core: Starfall Coronet + Air Burst Meteor, Tal Rasha's Iridescent Loop, Raiment of the Infinite. Maximum single-target burst.
builds.narrator: One meteor, one good look at the sky — same plan as always, it just hits harder this season. Meet **The Comet**.

builds.name: The Stillpoint
builds.core: Ice Shards + Shatter Frost Nova, Signet of Pelghain, Azurewrath, Hail of Verglas. AoE clear with real crowd control.
builds.narrator: Ice Shards won't freeze a thing on its own. Everything standing around it will. Meet **The Stillpoint**.

builds.name: The Overcharge
builds.core: Charged Bolts, Flame Shield (Flash), Unstable Currents (Boundless), Ceaseless Conduit. Sustained burst, forgiving.
builds.narrator: This one doesn't build up to a big hit. It just never stops hitting. Meet **The Overcharge**.

builds.name: The Storm Front
builds.core: Ball Lightning, Unstable Currents, Teleport, Harlequin Crest. High mobility, hit-and-run damage.
builds.narrator: Standing still is a mistake you don't plan on making twice. Not everyone's finished proving that yet, though. Meet **The Storm Front**.

builds.name: The Ember
builds.core: Incinerate (Flamethrower), Hydra, Familiar, Ice Armor, Teleport. Leveling 1-70, mana-efficient.
builds.narrator: Nobody needs the fancy version on day one. Meet **The Ember** — small, steady, and it doesn't ask much of you.

builds.name: The Wrecking Ball
builds.core: Whirlwind, Tibault's Will, Gohr's Devastating Grips, Ring of Starless Skies. Near one-button AoE clear.
builds.narrator: Splash damage, constant motion, never standing still long enough to get hit. Meet **The Wrecking Ball**. Whirlwind never stops. Neither do you.

builds.name: The War Council
builds.core: Challenging Shout, War Cry, Mighty Throw + Call of the Ancients double-ultimate loop. Highest sustained damage.
builds.narrator: You don't swing the biggest weapon here. You just tell the biggest things in the room to swing it for you. Meet **The War Council**.

builds.name: The Battering Ram
builds.core: Charge, Chainscourged Mail, Cremator's Aspect, Writhing Aspect. Fast, aggressive, movement-heavy.
builds.narrator: Some players want to hit once. You'd rather hit everything on the way there. Meet **The Battering Ram**.

builds.name: The Long Cut
builds.core: Rend + Leap, Wanton Rupture Aspect, Ring of the Ravenous, Shattered Vow. Kite and stack bleed.
builds.narrator: No big swing, no dramatic finish. Just a cut that keeps going long after you've moved on. Meet **The Long Cut**.

builds.name: The Anvil
builds.core: Hammer of the Ancients, generatorless, Aspect of Echoing Fury, Mantle of Mountain's Fury. Straightforward slam.
builds.narrator: Some things don't need reinventing. Meet **The Anvil** — one hammer, one generator-free rotation.

builds.name: The Blender
builds.core: Shred (Storm), Ifeh's Dire Totem, Malefic Crescent, Beru of the Storm Shepherd. Fast, mobile, tanky melee AoE.
builds.narrator: Some players want to hit hard. You'd rather hit everything, at once, while never really slowing down. Meet **The Blender**.

builds.name: The Downpour
builds.core: Lightning Storm, Azurewrath, Might of the Ursine, Storm Shepherd's Call. Highest damage ceiling in the class.
builds.narrator: Some players don't want to pick a side of the battlefield. Meet **The Downpour** — it isn't picky either.

builds.name: The Rockslide
builds.core: Boulder, Dolmen Stone, The Basilisk + Locran's Talisman, Beru of the Old Mountain. Orbiting persistent damage.
builds.narrator: You don't need to be fast if what you're carrying is heavy enough. Meet **The Rockslide**.

builds.name: The Twister
builds.core: Tornado, Beru of the Storm Shepherd, Ifeh's Dire Totem, Aspect of the Orange Herald. Familiar AoE flow.
builds.narrator: Some players would rather send several small disasters instead of one big one. Meet **The Twister**.

builds.name: The Landslide
builds.core: Pulverize, Werebear AoE smashing. Simple, forgiving, easy on-ramp.
builds.narrator: Not every hero needs six buttons and a rotation. Meet **The Landslide** — walk up, slam, repeat.

builds.name: The Red Tide
builds.core: Blood Wave (Hematolagnia), Kessime's Legacy, Ring of Starless Skies. Highest sustained AoE, simple to pilot.
builds.narrator: Some players don't want a plan. They want a wave that doesn't stop coming. Meet **The Red Tide**.

builds.name: The Pulse
builds.core: Blood Surge, Skeleton Warriors/Mages, Iron Maiden. Fast AoE clear, leveling and casual endgame.
builds.narrator: Nobody needs the wave yet. Just a pulse that clears the room. Meet **The Pulse**.

builds.name: The Quiet Harvest
builds.core: Skeletal Reapers, Shadow Mages, sacrificed Golem for crit. Hands-off, relaxed pacing, capped ceiling.
builds.narrator: Some players would rather watch. Meet **The Quiet Harvest** — you summon, you reposition, they do the rest.

builds.name: The Bodyguard
builds.core: Golem that never dies, Aspect of Reanimation, Aspect of Glynn's Anvil. Relaxed minion play, real boss burst.
builds.narrator: Some players want something between them and everything else. Meet **The Bodyguard**.

builds.name: The Orbit
builds.core: Bone Spirit, Astral Projection, Aspect of Bursting Bones, Crown of Lucion. Single-target and boss farming.
builds.narrator: One shape circling you, over and over, waiting for the right moment to go through something. Meet **The Orbit**.

builds.name: The Long Shot
builds.core: Penetrating Shot (Cold/Poison), Eaglehorn, Nilfur's Narrow Eye. Safest top-tier pick, ranged.
builds.narrator: Some players don't need to get close to be dangerous. Meet **The Long Shot**.

builds.name: The Flurry
builds.core: Dance of Knives, Death's Pavane, Beru of Spellbound Steel. Best leveling build, carries into endgame.
builds.narrator: Screen-wide, auto-targeting, and it doesn't slow down to aim. Meet **The Flurry**.

builds.name: The Boomerang
builds.core: Twisting Blades, Blade Shift, The Maestro, Etna's Lost Dagger. Stylish, mobile, DoT pressure.
builds.narrator: Throw it once. It comes back through everything on the way. Meet **The Boomerang**.

builds.name: The Quickdraw
builds.core: Rapid Fire, ricocheting arrows. Simple, low-risk, easy on-ramp.
builds.narrator: Simple, honest, and it doesn't ask you to think about imbuements yet. Meet **The Quickdraw**.

builds.name: The Locust
builds.core: Pestilent Swarm, Rushing Claw, Aspect of Infestation, Ring of Writhing Moon. Top speedfarming, high mobility.
builds.narrator: One of you becomes several of you, and none of them stay still long enough to hit. Meet **The Locust**.

builds.name: The Long Sting
builds.core: Stinger, Potent Sting, Harmony of Ebewaka, Rod of Kepeleke. Leveling through endgame, poison DoT.
builds.narrator: Not one hit. A slow spread, and then everything that's poisoned goes off at once. Meet **The Long Sting**.

builds.name: The Volley
builds.core: Quill Volley, Rod of Kepeleke, Reaper's Aspect. Dependable, always-solid ranged option.
builds.narrator: Nothing flashy. Just a volley that never really stops coming. Meet **The Volley**.

builds.name: The Flatten
builds.core: Crushing Hand, Rod of Kepeleke guaranteed crit, Prodigy's Tempo. Fast AoE, tanky, easy to pilot.
builds.narrator: Some players don't want to be clever about it. Meet **The Flatten** — walk in, hit the ground, walk out.

builds.name: The Judgment
builds.core: Wing Strikes, Aspect of Tyrael's Jurisdiction, Sanctis of Kethamar. Clean mix of damage and defense.
builds.narrator: Some players want the room to know judgment is coming before it lands. Meet **The Judgment**.

builds.name: The Fervor
builds.core: Zeal, Red Sermon, Embattled Aspect + Zealot Oath (near-full HP regen/sec). Fast, tanky melee.
builds.narrator: Glass cannon, sure — just make it out of diamond instead of glass. Meet **The Fervor**.

builds.name: The Bulwark
builds.core: Blessed Shield, Juggernaut Oath, Ward of the White Dove. Genuine tankiness with respectable damage.
builds.narrator: Some players would rather be the wall than get past it. Meet **The Bulwark**.

builds.name: The Hammerdin
builds.core: Blessed Hammer, cheap Faith cost, scales to endgame without a respec. Easiest strong leveling build.
builds.narrator: Some names carry over from a different game entirely, because the idea never needed replacing. Meet **The Hammerdin**.

builds.name: The Reckoning
builds.core: Apocalypse, Umbral Chains, Hands of the Worldbreaker, Fleshwrit Carapace. Highest ceiling, front-loaded complexity.
builds.narrator: You don't need this one to be fast. You need it to end the fight the moment it goes off. Meet **The Reckoning**.

builds.name: The Volunteer
builds.core: Lunatic (Command Fallen), Cage of Madness, Mega Lunatic split. Fast, easier AoE, low-effort farm.
builds.narrator: Something else walks into the blast for you. That's the whole plan. Meet **The Volunteer**.

builds.name: The Ambush
builds.core: Dread Claws, Shadowform stacks, Litany of Sable, Shadow of Harash set. Positioning burst, tanky enough.
builds.narrator: Stay hidden. Let them bunch up. Then it's not a fight anymore. Meet **The Ambush**.
